label: Task list
description: Help users understand the tasks required to complete a service and track their progress. Supports save-and-return for complex, multi-step services.
purpose: Give users visibility of all required tasks and their completion status, enabling pause and return.
use_when: The service involves multiple tasks, steps, or pages.
Users may need to pause and return later.
Tasks can be completed in a non-linear order.
do_not_use_when: The service can be completed in a single short linear flow.

inputs.label: Task definitions
inputs.description: List of tasks required to complete the service, with labels and ordering.

inputs.label: Current task statuses
inputs.description: Map of task IDs to current status: not-started, in-progress, completed.

outputs.label: Selected task ID
outputs.description: The task the user has chosen to start or continue.

outputs.label: All required tasks complete
outputs.description: True when all non-optional tasks are marked completed.

ux_considerations.text: Show a status label for each task (not started, in progress, completed) using both written labels and visual indicators — do not rely on colour alone.
Required tasks must be clearly indicated; optional tasks must be explicitly labelled as optional.
Users should only be blocked from submission if required tasks are incomplete.
Allow users to save progress and return later — restore accurate task statuses on return.
Labels should describe the activity, not reveal personal or sensitive data.

technical_notes.text: Task statuses must be persisted server-side, not only in client state.
Save-and-return must protect partially completed data appropriately.
Task completion state should be exposed as a structured output to enable agentic orchestration of multi-task services.
